ab: Maqtu caak pacingav su’un?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: Maqtu na,macial.
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: Maqtu caak pacingav su’un?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: Nii’i nitu madia ca qancaipun zaku.
en: B:Don't, I don't know much about it.

ab: Maiza iunlu’an suu ca qani’anin? maqtu azak munsu’uan tantungu?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I'd like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: Macial, naica’an azak lumaq maltatala su’un.
en: B: Okay, I'll wait for you at home.

ab: Maiza iunlu’an suu ca laqbing? asa caak munsu’uan pacingav is qaibasanan is bunun.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於布農族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I'd like to ask you about the legendary story of the [Bunun] people.

ab: Namunludun caak ca laqbing, uka’ caak ilumaq.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I'm going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won't be home.

ab: Tinan’a caak tu mantan asu maqaciap palcicia qaibasanan is bunun,asa azak pacingav su’un.
ch: A:我聽說您對布農族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you know a lot about the traditional customs of the Bunun, so I would like to ask you a few questions.

ab: Nitu naun madia ca qancaipun zaku, uutu natanam caak antalam su’un.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don't know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: Maqtu caak siza’ qaningu? Maupata nanitu inpulungan ca ickuinin.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: Uu na, nitu makua.
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: Piang ca qamisan suu haip?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: Masivaun qan vauin ca qamisan i naak.
en: B: I'm 98 years old.

ab: Talminduang asu ki.
en: A: You're so young.

ab: Nii na, madaingazin azak.
en: B: No, I'm not! I'm old.

ab: Mina’ica asu?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: Minaca’an azak qatu.
en: B: I'm from [CKS].

ab: Bununtuza amin ca dingalan suu?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是布農族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also [Bunun]?

ab: Nii, livaqlivaq ca dingalan naak.
en: B: No, she's a [Puyuma].

ab: Ma’az ca qancaipun su’u duma’ a qalinga.
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: Maqanciap azak bazbaz is malasbunu ciin livaqlivaq.
ch: B: 我會說布農語和卑南語。
en: B: I can speak [Bueno] and [Puyuma].

ab: Mantan azak manahip maqtu pacingav su’un, maqtu asu tanam palcicia itu qanup tu kitsamuan?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It's a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: Macial, matu talmindung azak a, daqanin caak munludun maqanup.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: Miqumicang, maca sintaqu’ suu haip a, tuza’ tu mantan macial.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: Maca sinbazbaz naak ka mu’amin tukutuku, iciang madadaingaz sinkakiv a qalinga.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: Qanaqtungin ca sinpacingav haip, mantan miqumicang is sintaqu suu.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That's all for today's interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: Nitu makua, namuqnang ickuinin paliqaciap.
en: B: You're welcome, let's talk again.

ab: Namatinghilun azak, maltatala ca ickuinin muqnang tan’a madia’ a qaibasanan.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I'm going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: Uu, kandaukdauka.
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
